label: Literary Themes
description: The abstract, philosophical, or emotional drivers that animate a work — the why and what does it mean questions the book is wrestling with.

tags.definition: Trust broken by someone close; the aftermath of disloyalty
Social hierarchy, mobility, and the systems that enforce inequality
Loss of innocence and the formation of adult identity
Mortality, grief, loss, the confrontation with finitude
Want, longing, obsession — the gap between what is and what is wanted
Obligation vs. personal will; the cost of loyalty to an external code
Predestination, inevitability, the tension between free will and determinism
Liberation, escape, the meaning and limits of autonomy
The construction, performance, and constraint of gendered identity
The processing of loss; mourning and its many forms
The weight of past actions; conscience and moral reckoning
Who we are, how we know, and how others define us
Purity, naivety, and the forces that corrupt or preserve it
Fairness, retribution, the failure or success of moral and legal systems
Romantic, familial, obsessive, sacrificial — the full range of attachment
How the past shapes the present; the reliability and distortion of recollection
The awareness of death as a shaping force on living
The relationship between humans and the natural world
Single-minded fixation and its consequences
Who has it, who doesn't, and what it does to people
Racism, racial identity, and systems of racial power
The possibility (or impossibility) of making amends or becoming better
Being cast out, unwanted, or denied belonging
The drive to repay harm; justice by one's own hand
What we give up — for love, duty, survival, others
The drive to persist in the face of annihilation
Honesty, deception, the elusiveness of objective reality
Its causes, effects, and moral meaning
Conflict between groups and what it reveals about human nature
